categories.name: Required Skill Areas
categories.description: Extract 2-5 specific technical skill domains demanded by the role. Use a controlled vocabulary in lowercase snake_case. Choose from: networking, kubernetes, gpu_programming, llm_inference, model_training, distributed_systems, data_engineering, frontend, mobile, security, compliance, physical_infrastructure, databases, observability, prompt_engineering, agent_development, pre_sales, government_clearance. If a skill isn't in this list but is highly prominent, create a concise snake_case tag for it.

categories.name: Primary Role Motion
categories.description: Identify the primary phase of the customer lifecycle this role operates in. Use 'pre_sales_focus' for roles driving technical wins before a deal closes. Use 'post_sales_implementation' for deploying existing products. Use 'zero_to_one_builder' for creating entirely new bespoke products for customers. Use 'field_hardware_ops' for physical deployment and maintenance.

categories.name: Deployment Environment
categories.description: Where the software or systems are primarily being deployed. Use 'customer_vpc_or_on_prem' for deploying into the customer's own secure infrastructure. Use 'managed_cloud_saas' for integrating with a vendor-hosted cloud platform. Use 'physical_hardware_or_edge' for deploying to physical devices, robots, or field hardware.

categories.name: Target Customer Industry
categories.description: The primary industry or sector of the customers this role serves. If not explicitly focused on a single vertical, use 'general_enterprise'.

categories.name: Travel Requirement
categories.description: The expected amount of travel to customer sites or field locations. Extract from percentages or descriptions. Use 'none_or_unstated' if travel is not mentioned.

categories.name: AI/ML Depth
categories.description: The level of AI/ML technical depth required for the role. Use 'model_training_and_tuning' if it requires PyTorch, fine-tuning, or GPU optimization. Use 'agent_and_prompt_engineering' if it requires LLM APIs, RAG, or LangChain. Use 'api_integration_only' for basic REST API usage without deep ML knowledge. Use 'not_ai_focused' for traditional software or infrastructure roles.

categories.name: Customer Engagement Depth
categories.description: How deeply the engineer embeds with the customer's team. Use 'deep_embed_months' if they act as a founding engineer/partner for the customer. Use 'project_based_weeks' for standard implementation/migration projects. Use 'transactional_or_advisory' for short pre-sales demos, support tickets, or architectural advice.

categories.name: Target User Persona
categories.description: The primary persona the engineer is building for or supporting at the customer organization.

categories.name: Solution Customization Level
categories.description: How much custom code the engineer writes per customer. Use 'bespoke_product_development' for building net-new applications from scratch. Use 'heavy_integration_and_glue_code' for writing scripts, data pipelines, and API connectors. Use 'configuration_and_light_scripting' for mostly UI configuration, standard SDK setup, or YAML.

categories.name: Programming Languages
categories.description: Specific programming languages explicitly required or strongly preferred in the listing. Return as lowercase strings. Return an empty list if none are specified.

categories.name: Software & Tool Familiarity
categories.description: Specific named products, SaaS services, named open-source projects, and vendor tools the listing mentions a candidate should be familiar with (as opposed to generic skill areas, which live in `required_skill_areas`). You MUST only use values from this exact list: 'airflow', 'ansible', 'claude', 'codex', 'cursor', 'databricks', 'datadog', 'docker', 'git', 'github', 'grafana', 'jira', 'kubernetes', 'linux', 'pytorch', 'react', 's3', 'slack', 'snowflake', 'terraform', 'v0', 'zendesk'. Items NOT in that list MUST be omitted. Return an empty list if no listed tool is explicitly named.

categories.name: Sales Quota or Commission
categories.description: True if the compensation or role description explicitly mentions a sales quota, commission, OTE (On-Target Earnings), or pre-sales revenue impact. False otherwise.

categories.name: Open Source Involvement
categories.description: True if the role explicitly involves contributing to, supporting, or leveraging open-source projects as a core responsibility. False otherwise.

categories.name: Requires Active Security Clearance
categories.description: True if the role explicitly requires an active government security clearance (e.g., Secret, TS/SCI) to apply or perform the job. False otherwise.
